existingSystem.description: Flutter mobile app + NestJS API. iter-2 (Event Discovery Filters) and iter-4 (AI Cover Image Generation) completed.

plan.summary: Five sequential iterations complete Rideglory's MVP. Iter-1 is a pure UI/UX Redesign pass covering 15 existing screens with no new features or backend changes — establishing a consistent design system baseline before new capabilities are layered on. Iter-2 delivers SOAT registration, FCM notification infrastructure, and the ManageAttendeesPage redesign (Story 2.9) deferred from iter-1. Iter-3 completes real-time tracking with SOS, organizer controls, background GPS, and the Mapbox SDK migration as explicit Story 3.0. Iter-4 activates the social layer with the follow system and complete public profiles, and provisions the deep link domain for iter-1. Iter-1 closes the release gate with Android App Links + iOS Universal Links event sharing, Apple Sign-In, and full notification tap routing. All FEEDBACK_REPLAN changes incorporated: Story 1.11 (ManageAttendeesPage) deferred from iter-1 to iter-2 as Story 2.9; iter-1 scope set to 15 screens (1.10 included, 1.11 deferred); auth frames gate added as iter-1 pre-flight item; module-scoped PRs (max 40 files) added to iter-1 DoD; 5 manual smoke tests added to iter-1 DoD; app_event_badge atom extraction added as pre-condition for Story 1.5; document slot pill molecule extraction added during Story 1.7; iter-1 title normalized to 'Deep Links + Apple Sign-In + Notification Routing'; iter-1 redesign regression risk added to risks.

frontend.styling: Dark theme, orange primary #f98c1f, Space Grotesk
frontend.rationale: Existing stack confirmed — Clean Architecture, Retrofit, GetIt/Injectable, go_router all proven and correctly established. No refactoring needed. Iter-1 is presentation-layer only: no code generation, no domain changes, no DI changes.

backend.framework: NestJS microservices (api-gateway + events-ms, vehicles-ms, users-ms, maintenances-ms)
backend.rationale: Existing microservices architecture is sound. FCM dispatch stays in api-gateway (firebase-admin already installed). @nestjs/schedule added for SOAT and maintenance cron jobs in iter-2.

database.rationale: Existing per-MS Prisma schemas. New entities in iter-2: Soat (vehicles-ms), Notification (api-gateway — first-time Prisma setup). fcmToken field added to User (users-ms). New entity in iter-4: Follow (users-ms).

assumptions.text: mapbox_maps_flutter is absent from pubspec.yaml and pubspec.lock — it has never been installed. google_maps_flutter ^2.10.0 is the live SDK. The migration in Story 3.0 is a real task (4 Dart files + 4 native files), not a documentation cleanup.
assumptions.impact: Story 3.0 is the first story of iter-3 and a hard blocker for all other iter-3 stories. Estimated 1-2 days of work for an experienced Flutter developer.

assumptions.text: Firebase Dynamic Links is EOL (August 2025) and will not be used. Iter-1 uses app_links + Android App Links + iOS Universal Links instead.
assumptions.impact: A custom domain must be provisioned during iter-4 (not iter-1). assetlinks.json and apple-app-site-association must be live before iter-1 begins.

assumptions.text: FCM background message handler runs in a separate Dart isolate; DI (GetIt) must be re-initialized inside the handler. @pragma('vm:entry-point') annotation is required on the top-level handler function.
assumptions.impact: Pattern established in iter-2. Failure to implement correctly causes silent missed notifications on some devices.

assumptions.text: Notification read/unread state is stored in the backend (notifications table in api-gateway). SharedPreferences may be used only as a local badge-count cache for read optimization.
assumptions.impact: Story 2.8 must be complete before Story 2.7 can be fully implemented. Testing order: 2.8 -> 2.7 -> 2.4/2.5/2.6.

assumptions.text: api-gateway has no Prisma schema (no prisma/ directory exists). Adding Prisma is a first-time setup: prisma init + schema creation + prisma migrate dev. This is different from the prisma migrate reset run on the 4 existing microservices.
assumptions.impact: Allot a full pre-flight day for api-gateway Prisma initialization. Docker Compose DB network and DATABASE_URL must be configured before migrate dev can run.

assumptions.text: iOS persistent notification for background GPS (iter-3) is the system-managed blue location indicator in the status bar. A custom notification text ('Rideglory — Rodada activa') is Android-only (foreground service). iOS users will see different behavior than Android users.
assumptions.impact: UX discrepancy between platforms is acceptable for MVP and must be communicated via onboarding copy or in-app tooltip.

assumptions.text: Apple Sign-In requires manual Xcode configuration (entitlement com.apple.developer.applesignin, Apple Developer Portal setup, Firebase Console Apple provider). Flutter tooling cannot automate this step.
assumptions.impact: Iter-1 pre-flight must include this configuration step. Allow 1 business day for provisioning profile propagation.

assumptions.text: Route polyline should be stored as GeoJSON LineString (routeGeoJson Json?) in events-ms, not as a Google-encoded polyline string as specified in PRD §17.2. prisma migrate reset discards all existing event data so no legacy format migration is needed.
assumptions.impact: Backend developer must coordinate GeoJSON contract with Flutter developer before iter-3 tracking stories begin.

assumptions.text: Iter-1 is presentation-layer only. A grep of lib/features/*/presentation/ shows only 3 imports of core/data/ files, all referencing colombia_motos_brands_data.dart (a static in-app catalog). No presentation code imports feature DTOs or services. No code generation files need regeneration.
assumptions.impact: Iter-1 can proceed without backend coordination. No domain model or data layer changes permitted during iter-1.

risks.text: Pre-flight for iter-1 takes longer than expected — auth frames missing in rideglory.pen, donut chart scope ambiguity, 15-screen gap analysis — delaying code start.
risks.mitigation: Enforce design gate strictly: no Flutter code until gap analysis is complete and reviewed. Parallelize: Designer inspects Pencil while Developer runs dart analyze baseline on main. Auth frames gate is per-story (blocks Story 1.3 only, not 1.2 or 1.4–1.10).

risks.text: Iter-4 AI cover generation widget breaks during event form refactor (Story 1.6).
risks.mitigation: Mandatory smoke test: generate cover → select image → save event → confirm functional. Treat as a blocking acceptance criterion. Documented in iter-1 DoD.

risks.text: 95–135 file diff across 6 modules is unreviewable as a single PR.
risks.mitigation: Split into 5–6 module-scoped PRs (max 40 files each) merged into a feature branch. dart analyze + flutter test green required before each module PR merges.

risks.text: Iter-1 redesign causes regressions in existing functionality (iter-4 AI cover, event form Mapbox route preview, maintenance donut chart, CTA state variants).
risks.mitigation: 5 manual smoke tests required before final merge: AI cover generation, CTA state variants, donut chart, bottom nav pill bar, Mapbox route preview. All 10 existing tests must pass green after each module PR.

risks.text: Background GPS foreground service (Android flutter_foreground_task + IsolateNameServer bridge) does not work correctly on devices with aggressive battery restrictions (Xiaomi, Samsung) and cannot be tested in the emulator.
risks.mitigation: Mandate physical device testing from day 1 of iter-3. Write separate platform test plans for Android and iOS.

risks.text: Deep link domain unresolved — iter-1 cannot start without assetlinks.json and apple-app-site-association live on a domain with valid TLS.
risks.mitigation: Explicit iter-4 action item (not a story). Must be provisioned and verified with curl before iter-4 closes. Hard stop.

risks.text: api-gateway Prisma first-time setup (Docker Compose DB network, DATABASE_URL configuration, potential port conflicts with existing services) more complex than the 1-hour estimate.
risks.mitigation: Allot full pre-flight day. Document the exact DATABASE_URL and Docker Compose changes in the iter-2 pre-flight runbook.

risks.text: Story 3.0 (Mapbox migration) consumes the full iter-3 sprint and blocks all other stories.
risks.mitigation: Story 3.0 is already gated as iter-blocking. Widget test for route_map_preview.dart required before 3.0 PR can merge. Stories 3.1–3.7 are explicitly blocked on 3.0 merge.

risks.text: GoRouter is not currently registered in GetIt. NotificationRouteHandler (iter-1) needs a router reference and may require refactoring app_router.dart.
risks.mitigation: GoRouter DI assessment is a DoD item in iter-4. If refactoring is needed, scope as Story 1.0 pre-flight.

risks.text: Mapbox iOS binary framework download (~200MB via SPM/Cocoapods) adds 5-10 minutes per CI run after Story 3.0 merge.
risks.mitigation: DevOps must update Cocoapods cache key in GitHub Actions immediately after Story 3.0 is merged.

deferred.reason: Alta complejidad (ML Kit / Cloud Vision); entrada manual es suficiente para MVP.

deferred.item: Recordatorio de mantenimiento por km (odómetro estimado)
deferred.reason: Requiere sistema de tracking de odómetro automático no definido en el PRD.
EOL agosto 2025. Reemplazado por app_links + Android App Links + iOS Universal Links en iter-1.

deferred.item: Dynamic Links para perfiles de riders
deferred.reason: Bajo impacto vs. esfuerzo; los eventos son el caso de uso crítico de sharing.
Sin demanda validada para MVP; el discovery es por búsqueda directa.
Descartado por decisión del human reviewer — fuente de verdad es el backend.
Complejidad legal; el cobro es externo al MVP.

deferred.item: Verificación automática de SOAT via RUNT API
Feed social de publicaciones / fotos de rodadas

deferred.reason: Fase posterior; MVP es solo español.

iterations.notes: Initial project setup. iter-2 and iter-4 completed prior to state.json initialization.

iterations.goal: UI/UX Redesign: bring 15 existing screens into alignment with rideglory.pen design — no new features, no backend changes. Establishes consistent design system baseline before new capabilities are layered on.
iterations.notes: Pre-flight: gap analysis and Pencil frame inspection BEFORE any Flutter code. Auth frames gate: Story 1.3 blocked until Login/Signup/PasswordRecovery frames confirmed in rideglory.pen; Stories 1.2 and 1.4–1.10 may proceed in parallel. app_event_badge.dart atom must be extracted before Story 1.5 begins. Document slot pill (aGqnv) extracted as molecule during Story 1.7. Implementation split into 5–6 module-scoped PRs (max 40 files each). 5 manual smoke tests required before final merge. Story 1.10 (RegistrationFormPage + RegistrationDetailPage) is included. Story 1.11 (ManageAttendeesPage) deferred to iter-2 as Story 2.9.
iterations.definitionOfDone:
- [ ] Design gate: all 15 screen frames inspected/confirmed in rideglory.pen via Pencil MCP before any Flutter code changes begin; gap analysis complete and reviewed
- [ ] Auth frames gate satisfied: Pencil frames for Login, Signup, Password Recovery confirmed or created in rideglory.pen before Story 1.3 implementation begins
- [ ] Implementation split into 5–6 module-scoped PRs (max 40 files each): splash+auth, home, events, garage, maintenance, registration
- [ ] Each module PR: dart analyze + flutter test green before merge into feature branch
- [ ] No single PR exceeds 40 files changed
- [ ] All Color(0x...) and Colors.<named> hardcoded literals in lib/features/ replaced with colorScheme or AppColors constants
- [ ] All ElevatedButton → AppButton; raw TextFormField → AppTextField; raw AlertDialog → AppDialog
- [ ] dart analyze passes with zero new violations on the final feature branch
- [ ] All 10 existing flutter test cases pass — zero regressions
- [ ] app_event_badge.dart atom extracted from frame zKkmE before Story 1.5 implementation begins
- [ ] Document slot pill (aGqnv) extracted as design system molecule during Story 1.7
- [ ] AI cover generation widget (iter-4) remains functional — mandatory smoke test: generate cover → select image → save event → confirm functional
- [ ] 5 manual smoke tests passed before final merge: (a) AI cover generation, (b) Event detail CTA state variants, (c) Maintenance donut chart rendering, (d) Home bottom nav pill bar, (e) Mapbox route preview in event form
- [ ] Bottom navigation pill bar (VMmN0) verified to match frame exactly across all shell screens
- [ ] All 3 events widget tests updated in the same PR that swaps their widgets — no test-rot merges
- [ ] app_es.arb updated for any UI copy changes; flutter gen-l10n run and generated files committed
- [ ] No new backend endpoints; no new domain models; no new use cases; no new routes introduced

stories.story: As a designer reviewing existing screens, I can identify all visual gaps between the current implementation and rideglory.pen frames, documented in a gap analysis.
stories.acceptance: Gap analysis document lists every screen (all 15) with specific mismatches: component name, spacing value, color token, typography rule. Document reviewed and approved before any Flutter code changes begin.

stories.story: As a rider opening the app, I see the splash screen with the correct logo, loading state indicator, and overall layout matching the rideglory.pen design.
stories.acceptance: Splash screen layout, logo sizing, background color, and loading indicator match the Pencil frame exactly. All loading states (loading, error, success) handled visually. No hardcoded colors.

stories.story: As a rider on the auth screens (login, signup, password recovery), the pages use AppButton, AppTextField, AppPasswordTextField, correct typography, and the exact color tokens from the design system.
stories.acceptance: No ElevatedButton, no TextFormField direct usage, no hardcoded color literals on any auth screen. AppButton used for all primary and secondary actions. Space Grotesk applied. Password recovery confirmation screen matches design. Auth frames gate must be satisfied before this story begins.

stories.story: As a rider on the Home Dashboard, the layout matches the rideglory.pen frame dyWWs — including the greeting header, garage card (main vehicle + empty state), upcoming rides section (horizontal scroll + empty state), and bottom navigation pill bar.
stories.acceptance: Frame dyWWs matched: correct spacing, correct card border radius, correct color tokens. Bottom nav pill bar matches frame VMmN0. SOAT badge placeholder not included (iter-2). No layout regressions vs. current behavior.

stories.story: As a rider browsing Events, the events list page and event detail page match the rideglory.pen frames Neipf and kAubW — including event cards, badges (zKkmE), filter chips, the filter bottom sheet, and the CTA bar on event detail. Pre-condition: lib/design_system/atoms/app_event_badge.dart must be extracted/created from frame zKkmE before this story's implementation begins.
stories.acceptance: Event list: search bar, filter chips, event cards (image overlay, badge, organizer avatar, chips) match frame Neipf. Event detail: hero image, metric chips, map preview, allowed brands chips, CTA bar match frame kAubW. Filter bottom sheet layout correct. app_event_badge.dart used in all event card contexts.

stories.story: As a rider creating an event, the Create/Edit Event form matches the rideglory.pen frame zbCa0 — correct input fields, layout sections, difficulty selector, and button styles.
stories.acceptance: Form layout matches frame zbCa0. AI cover generation widget (iter-4 feature) is preserved and functional. Mapbox route preview widget unchanged. All inputs use AppTextField. AppButton used for primary actions.

stories.story: As a rider viewing the Garage, the vehicle list page and vehicle detail page match rideglory.pen frames KCf6W and P1GSzZ — including the main vehicle card, 'other vehicles' list, spec chips, and document slots. Note: document slot pill (aGqnv) should be extracted as a design system molecule during this story.
stories.acceptance: Vehicle list matches frame KCf6W: main vehicle card with full-width image, stats chips, quick-access buttons; compact other-vehicles list. Vehicle detail matches frame P1GSzZ: specs, document badges, action buttons. All states (loading, empty, data, error) visually correct.

stories.story: As a rider adding or editing a vehicle, the Add/Edit vehicle form matches rideglory.pen frame EqnMm — correct field layout, image upload UI, and step structure.
stories.acceptance: Form fields, image upload banner, and section layout match frame EqnMm. Document slot section (SOAT, tech review) UI is present but non-functional pending iter-2. AppTextField and AppButton used throughout.

stories.story: As a rider viewing Maintenance, the dashboard, history list, and new maintenance forms match rideglory.pen frames Ako7u (dashboard), SykjL (history), J5h6P (step 1), eK2WW (step 2 — completed), and ELB5u (step 2 — scheduled).
stories.acceptance: All 5 maintenance frames matched. Dashboard: donut chart health indicator, urgency color coding (red/yellow/green) correct. History: year grouping, cost summary, chronological order. Filters bottom sheet (frame v6RqaX) layout correct. Step 1 grid 2x4 card layout correct. Step 2 tab (Completado / Programado) layout correct.

stories.story: As a rider viewing their registrations, the My Registrations list and Registration Detail pages match rideglory.pen frames oUv12 and the registration list layout.
stories.acceptance: Registration list and detail pages use design system components throughout; no hardcoded colors; empty and loading states correct.

iterations.architectNotes: Iter-1 is presentation-layer only. Confirmed: only 3 imports of core/data/ files in lib/features/*/presentation/, all referencing colombia_motos_brands_data.dart (static catalog). No domain models, DTOs, services, or code generation files need changes. File blast radius: ~95–135 files touched. Heavy lift is color tokenization (~33 raw Color(0x...) literals + ~80 files with Colors.<named> references), not widget swapping. Component adoption already mature (only 3 files with ElevatedButton/TextFormField usage).
iterations.designNotes: app_event_badge.dart atom must be extracted from frame zKkmE BEFORE Story 1.5 begins — not a DoD checkbox but a named pre-condition task in gap analysis. Document slot pill (aGqnv) extracted as molecule during Story 1.7 for reuse in iter-2 SOAT badge. Auth frame gap: if no Login/Signup/PasswordRecovery frames exist in rideglory.pen, Designer creates them before Story 1.3 begins. Donut chart (Ako7u): Designer must flag during pre-flight whether donut chart is 'swap colors only' or requires geometry/animation changes — if the latter, descope to colors-only for iter-1.
iterations.techLeadNotes: All 3 events widget tests (attendees_list_navigation_test.dart, event_filters_bottom_sheet_test.dart, events_page_view_test.dart) will need finder updates when widgets are swapped — these updates are part of the story, not post-merge cleanup. Module-scoped PR strategy is mandatory: 5–6 PRs into a feature branch, each requiring dart analyze + flutter test green before merge. Maximum 40 files per PR. No test-rot merges allowed.

iterations.goal: SOAT + Notification Foundation: permitir a los riders registrar y rastrear su SOAT por vehículo, y recibir notificaciones push para los eventos más críticos. Establece la infraestructura FCM y la tabla de notificaciones en el backend que iter-3, iter-4 e iter-1 requieren. Incluye Story 2.9 (ManageAttendeesPage redesign) diferida de iter-1.
iterations.notes: FCM token registration debe ser la primera story de backend. Home Dashboard SOAT badge se implementa en iter-3. Notificaciones 2.4/2.5/2.6: solo recepción y visualización — navegación al tocar en iter-1. Testing order: 2.8 -> 2.7 -> 2.4/2.5/2.6. Notification pagination is cursor-based throughout (not offset/limit). POST /api/notifications/fcm-token is load-bearing for stories 2.4, 2.5, 2.6. Story 2.9: frame dUc9h must be confirmed to cover list + edit view — if ambiguous, descope to component-swap-only with no layout rework.
iterations.definitionOfDone:
- [ ] Design gate: SOAT upload, SOAT manual form, SOAT status detail, notification center frames in rideglory.pen — including vehicle detail page with 4-state badge and generic notification row template with icon slot — before implementation; frame dUc9h confirmed for Story 2.9 scope
- [ ] Pre-flight verified: seed.ts in vehicles-ms and events-ms; prisma migrate reset on 4 services; prisma init + prisma migrate dev on api-gateway; GET /api/vehicles returns 200 empty list
- [ ] Backend: POST /api/vehicles/:vehicleId/soat and GET /api/vehicles/:vehicleId/soat in vehicles-ms
- [ ] Backend: POST /api/notifications/fcm-token operative; users-ms User model has fcmToken String? field
- [ ] Backend: notifications table in api-gateway Prisma; GET /api/notifications (cursor ?cursor=<lastId>&limit=20), PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read, PATCH /api/notifications/read-all with Firebase Auth guard
- [ ] Backend: FCM push trigger in registration approval/rejection flow; each push inserts row in notifications table
- [ ] Backend: @nestjs/schedule with ScheduleModule.forRoot() in api-gateway AppModule; NotificationSchedulerService with @Cron for SOAT (30d, 7d, day-of); cron expressions use America/Bogota timezone
- [ ] Flutter: lib/features/soat/ with domain (SoatModel), data (SoatDto, SoatService), presentation (SoatCubit, SoatUploadPage, SoatManualFormPage, SoatStatusPage)
- [ ] Flutter: lib/features/notifications/ with domain (NotificationModel), data (NotificationsService with cursor pagination), presentation (NotificationsCubit, NotificationCenterPage)
- [ ] Flutter: NotificationsCubit loads from GET /api/notifications (cursor); markRead(id) calls PATCH; markAllRead() calls read-all
- [ ] FCM initialized in AuthCubit post-login: token registration + permission request; background message handler as top-level function with @pragma('vm:entry-point') and DI re-init documented
- [ ] flutter_local_notifications configured for iOS foreground banners; Android notification channel configured
- [ ] 6 notification types configured and tested on real device or emulator
- [ ] dart analyze clean; app_es.arb updated with all new strings
- [ ] Unit tests: SOAT badge logic (4 states); NotificationsCubit — initial load, cursor pagination, markRead, markAllRead
- [ ] Widget tests: SoatUploadPage, SoatManualFormPage, NotificationCenterPage
- [ ] Story 2.9 (ManageAttendeesPage): design system components used throughout; no hardcoded colors; loading/empty/error states correct
- [ ] Scope reduction rule: if 2.7 read-persistence is at risk, simplify to local badge reset with follow-up task; backend endpoints (2.8) must be complete
- [ ] Home Dashboard SOAT badge NOT included in this iteration — added in iter-3

stories.story: Como rider, puedo subir el documento de mi SOAT (foto o PDF desde galería/cámara) para un vehículo de mi garaje y guardar los datos (número de póliza, fechas, aseguradora) en el backend.
stories.acceptance: El documento queda guardado; el badge del vehículo cambia a 'Vigente' o 'Por vencer' según la fecha de vencimiento.

stories.story: Como rider, puedo ingresar manualmente los datos de mi SOAT (número de póliza, fecha inicio, fecha vencimiento, aseguradora) cuando no quiero subir el documento.
stories.acceptance: El formulario valida que la fecha de vencimiento sea obligatoria; al guardar el estado SOAT refleja la lógica de vigencia correcta.

stories.story: Como rider, veo en el detalle de mi vehículo un badge de estado SOAT (Sin SOAT / Vigente / Por vencer / Vencido) y puedo tocar el badge para ir al flujo de SOAT.
stories.acceptance: Los cuatro estados se calculan correctamente con base en la fecha de vencimiento vs. hoy; el badge es tap-able y navega al flujo correcto.

stories.story: Como rider, recibo una notificación push 30 días antes, 7 días antes y el día del vencimiento de mi SOAT con el nombre de la moto afectada.
stories.acceptance: Las tres notificaciones llegan al dispositivo en las fechas correctas y se muestran en el centro de notificaciones. (Navegación al tocar: iter-1.)

stories.story: Como organizador de evento, recibo una notificación push cuando un nuevo rider se inscribe a mi evento.
stories.acceptance: La notificación aparece en el centro de notificaciones; el badge de no leídas en el ícono de campana se incrementa. (Navegación al tocar: iter-1.)

stories.story: Como rider inscrito, recibo una notificación push cuando mi inscripción es aprobada o rechazada.
stories.acceptance: La notificación llega dentro de los 30 segundos de la acción del organizador; el estado en 'Mis inscripciones' ya refleja el cambio al abrir la pantalla. (Navegación al tocar: iter-1.)

stories.story: Como rider, puedo abrir el centro de notificaciones desde el ícono de campana del Home y ver todas mis notificaciones, diferenciando las no leídas (punto naranja) de las leídas. Al tocar una notificación o usar 'Marcar todas como leídas', el estado se persiste en el backend.
stories.acceptance: La lista muestra notificaciones cargadas desde el backend con paginación cursor (?cursor=<lastId>&limit=20, respuesta { data, nextCursor }); marcar como leído llama a PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read; 'Marcar todas' llama a PATCH /api/notifications/read-all; badge del ícono refleja conteo de no leídas desde backend; empty state 'Aún no tienes notificaciones' visible.

stories.story: Como desarrollador, el backend persiste todas las notificaciones en una tabla notifications en api-gateway y expone endpoints para listarlas, marcarlas como leídas y registrar el token FCM.
stories.acceptance: GET /api/notifications?cursor=<lastId>&limit=20 retorna { data: Notification[], nextCursor: string | null } del usuario autenticado, ordenadas por createdAt desc; PATCH /api/notifications/:id/read actualiza isRead = true; PATCH /api/notifications/read-all actualiza todas las no leídas del usuario; POST /api/notifications/fcm-token recibe { fcmToken: string }, actualiza el campo fcmToken String? en el modelo User de users-ms, es llamado desde AuthCubit post-login; los cuatro endpoints requieren Bearer token con guard de Firebase Auth; tabla notifications con campos id, userId, type, payload (JSON), isRead, createdAt.

stories.story: As an event organizer, the attendees management page (frame dUc9h) matches rideglory.pen design — using design system components, correct color tokens, and consistent loading/empty states.
stories.acceptance: ManageAttendeesPage uses AppButton, AppTextField, AppDialog throughout; no hardcoded color literals; loading, empty, and error states visually correct per Pencil frame dUc9h. If frame dUc9h covers list + edit, full layout is implemented. If frame covers edit only, scope is limited to component-swap and color tokenization (no layout rework without a confirmed frame).

iterations.architectNotes: api-gateway Prisma initialization is first-time setup (prisma init + prisma migrate dev), NOT reset. Distinct from the 4 existing microservices. Notifications table lives in api-gateway (no new microservice). firebase-admin messaging added to api-gateway — no new package needed. @nestjs/schedule cron expressions must use America/Bogota timezone. fcmToken field added to users-ms User model. Story 2.9 is presentation-layer only — same constraints as iter-1.
iterations.designNotes: SOAT status badge: 4 states (Sin SOAT / Vigente / Por vencer / Vencido) must be confirmed in Pencil frame for vehicle detail page. Notification center: define generic notification row template with icon slot for 6 notification types before implementing Story 2.7. Story 2.9: confirm frame dUc9h scope (list + edit, or edit only) during design gate.
iterations.techLeadNotes: @pragma('vm:entry-point') annotation required on top-level FCM background handler. DI re-initialization (configureDependencies()) inside handler is mandatory and must be in code review checklist. Notification pagination is cursor-based throughout — offset/limit must not be used. Story 2.9 scope depends on frame dUc9h confirmation: if list is not in the frame, limit to component-swap only.

iterations.goal: Tracking Completo + SOS + Maintenance Reminders: completar la experiencia de rastreo con botón SOS, controles del organizador, GPS en background, recordatorios de mantenimiento basados en fecha, y migración completa a mapbox_maps_flutter como único SDK de mapas (Story 3.0).
iterations.notes: Story 3.0 (Mapbox migration) is the first story and a hard blocker for all other stories — no pre-flight checklist for Maps SDK. Date-based maintenance reminders only (no km). Home Dashboard SOAT badge added in this iteration. Route stored as GeoJSON LineString (routeGeoJson Json?) in events-ms — not encoded polyline.
iterations.definitionOfDone:
- [ ] Design gate: frame qonbS in Pencil confirmed with SOS button + confirmation dialog, organizer control bar (conditionally visible), red pulsing SOS marker, Android foreground service notification text, empty state for 'no phone' in story 3.2
- [ ] Story 3.0 merged and dart analyze clean before any other story begins
- [ ] Zero google_maps_flutter or geocoding imports in lib/ (verified by grep in PR review)
- [ ] iOS Cocoapods cache updated in CI/CD after 3.0 merge
- [ ] Backend: POST /api/events/:eventId/tracking/start and POST /api/events/:eventId/tracking/end in api-gateway/src/tracking/
- [ ] Backend: sos handler in TrackingGateway — broadcast sos_alert via WebSocket + FCM multicast; deduplication guard with sosTriggeredAt in events-ms
- [ ] Backend: GET /api/events/:eventId/route in events-ms returning routeGeoJson (GeoJSON LineString, routeGeoJson Json? field on Event model)
- [ ] Backend: scheduler entries for maintenance (30d date-based) and 24h event reminder; America/Bogota timezone
- [ ] Flutter: route rendered with GeoJsonSource + LineLayer (NOT PolylineAnnotationManager); GeoJSON coordinates from backend
- [ ] Flutter: route adherence chip (En ruta / Fuera de ruta) with 200m Haversine check client-side over GeoJSON coordinates
- [ ] Flutter: SOS button + confirmation dialog; SOS banner with Llamar/Localizar; red pulsing marker with mapbox_maps_flutter annotations API
- [ ] Flutter: organizer Iniciar/Terminar controls in EventDetailPage and EventTrackingPage; LiveTrackingCubit emits TrackingFinished on tracking.event.ended WebSocket event
- [ ] Flutter: flutter_foreground_task on Android (foreground service isolate + IsolateNameServer; configureDependencies() called in onStart()); geolocator with AppleSettings(activityType: ActivityType.automotiveNavigation) on iOS
- [ ] Flutter: VehicleModel with soatStatus and soatExpiryDate; Home Dashboard SOAT badge on main vehicle card
- [ ] dart analyze clean; FOREGROUND_SERVICE + FOREGROUND_SERVICE_LOCATION permissions in AndroidManifest.xml reviewed against Play Store policy
- [ ] Physical device test for background GPS (Android foreground service + iOS background location) — logs attached to PR
- [ ] Separate Android/iOS manual test plans for background GPS
- [ ] app_es.arb updated; location usage descriptions in Info.plist in clear Spanish

stories.story: Como desarrollador, el proyecto usa mapbox_maps_flutter como único SDK de mapas. google_maps_flutter y geocoding han sido eliminados completamente.
stories.acceptance: pubspec.yaml declara mapbox_maps_flutter ^2.2.0; google_maps_flutter y geocoding eliminados. dart analyze pasa con cero errores o warnings en lib/. Sin imports de google_maps_flutter o geocoding en lib/. live_map_widget.dart, live_map_page.dart, initials_marker_icon.dart y route_map_preview.dart compilan y renderizan correctamente en device físico. route_map_preview.dart usa PlaceService (Retrofit async) para lookup de dirección; estados loading y error manejados con patrón ResultState. AndroidManifest.xml contiene Mapbox token meta-data; Info.plist contiene MBXAccessToken; ninguna API key de Google Maps permanece en native config. iOS Cocoapods install completado; app builds en simulator.

stories.story: Como rider en una rodada activa, puedo presionar el botón SOS rojo visible en el mapa, confirmar la alerta en un diálogo, y todos los demás riders de la rodada reciben una notificación push de emergencia con mi nombre y ubicación.
stories.acceptance: La alerta SOS se procesa en menos de 5 segundos; el marcador en el mapa de todos los participantes cambia a rojo pulsante; aparece un banner rojo con el nombre del rider en crisis.

stories.story: Como rider que ve una alerta SOS en el mapa, puedo tocar el banner del rider en crisis y acceder a Llamar (dialer nativo) y Localizar (Google Maps / Apple Maps con navegación).
stories.acceptance: Las dos acciones funcionales en iOS y Android; el teléfono del rider solo aparece si lo tiene registrado; si no tiene teléfono, solo se muestra Localizar.

stories.story: Como organizador de un evento, puedo iniciar la rodada desde la pantalla de detalle del evento, cambiando el estado a in_progress y habilitando el rastreo para todos los inscritos aprobados.
stories.acceptance: Botón 'Iniciar rodada' solo visible para el organizador; al confirmar, estado actualizado en backend; riders ven CTA 'Ver rastreo' en detalle del evento.

stories.story: Como organizador de un evento activo, puedo terminar la rodada desde la pantalla de rastreo, cambiando el estado a finished y cerrando la pantalla para todos.
stories.acceptance: Estado cambia a finished; pantalla de rastreo cierra en todos los dispositivos conectados al WebSocket; push 'La rodada ha terminado' llega en menos de 10s.

stories.story: Como rider en una rodada activa con app en background, el app sigue enviando mi ubicación al WebSocket cada 5 segundos. Android: notificación persistente no descartable 'Rideglory — Rodada activa'. iOS: indicador de ubicación azul del sistema.
stories.acceptance: Android: ubicación actualiza con app en background, foreground service visible y no descartable (device físico requerido). iOS: ubicación actualiza, indicador sistema visible (comportamiento nativo, device físico requerido).

stories.story: Como rider con un mantenimiento programado, recibo una push 30 días antes de la fecha programada. (Solo fecha — sin km.)
stories.acceptance: Push generada al guardar mantenimiento con fecha futura; texto incluye tipo de servicio y nombre de moto.

stories.story: Como rider inscrito y aprobado a un evento, recibo una push de recordatorio 24 horas antes del evento.
stories.acceptance: Push llega entre 23h 55min y 24h 5min antes de la hora de inicio del evento.

iterations.architectNotes: Route polyline must be stored as GeoJSON LineString (routeGeoJson Json? field on Event model in events-ms). Render on map using GeoJsonSource + LineLayer — NOT PolylineAnnotationManager. Route adherence (200m check) computed client-side using Haversine formula over GeoJSON coordinate array — no Mapbox decoder library needed. Mapbox token goes into AndroidManifest.xml and Info.plist natively (public token starting pk. — safe to commit). configureDependencies() must be called inside flutter_foreground_task handler's onStart() method.
iterations.designNotes: Story 3.0 migration is invisible to users if done correctly. SOS button placement, organizer control bar visibility rules, and red pulsing marker animation spec must be confirmed in frame qonbS before implementation. Empty state for 'rider has no phone number' in story 3.2 must be specified (show only Localizar button).
iterations.techLeadNotes: Story 3.0 is the highest-risk item: 4 Dart files + 4 native/config files. route_map_preview.dart geocoding replacement is the most architecturally complex piece — sync call becomes debounced async Retrofit call with ResultState error handling. Widget test required. iOS Cocoapods rebuild with ~200MB Mapbox binary framework is a CI time bomb — DevOps must update cache key immediately after 3.0 merges.

iterations.goal: Sistema de Seguidores + Perfil Completo: activar el sistema social básico con seguir/dejar de seguir, listas de seguidores/siguiendo, y completar las pantallas de perfil. Provisionar el dominio de deep links durante esta iteración (bloqueante para iter-1).
iterations.notes: Story 4.5: solo recepción y visualización — navegación al tocar en iter-1. FollowCubit usa estado optimista con @freezed FollowState (excepción documentada al patrón ResultState<T>). Design gate: frame A7qDd (Profile) debe tener diseño final antes de implementación. ACTION ITEM during iter-4 (hard blocker for iter-1): provision custom domain for Android App Links + iOS Universal Links — TLS valid, assetlinks.json + apple-app-site-association deployed and publicly reachable. GoRouter DI assessment required as DoD item.
iterations.definitionOfDone:
- [ ] Design gate: frame A7qDd (Profile) updated to final design in rideglory.pen; FollowersListPage and FollowingListPage frames created — including follow button in loading/in-flight state, quick-follow in list, empty states
- [ ] Deep link domain provisioned: assetlinks.json and apple-app-site-association accessible publicly at chosen domain; valid TLS; verified with curl before iter-4 closes
- [ ] Backend: Follow entity (followerId, followingId, createdAt, composite unique index) in users-ms Prisma; POST /api/users/:userId/follow, DELETE /api/users/:userId/follow, GET /api/users/:userId/followers?page=&limit=, GET /api/users/:userId/following?page=&limit=
- [ ] Backend: _count.followers and _count.following included in user profile response
- [ ] Backend: GET /api/vehicles?userId=:userId returns PublicVehicleDto (separate class from VehicleDto, without licensePlate or insurance fields)
- [ ] Backend: FCM push 'nuevo seguidor' dispatched from api-gateway post-proxy; row inserted in notifications table
- [ ] Flutter: FollowCubit with optimistic state (@freezed FollowState { isFollowing, followerCount, isLoading, error }); registered as factory with userId parameter in DI (exception documented)
- [ ] Flutter: FollowersListPage and FollowingListPage with offset/limit load-more pagination and explicit empty states
- [ ] Flutter: complete profile with bio, ciudad, real counters, public vehicles (via PublicVehicleDto), organized events
- [ ] NEW_FOLLOWER type handled in NotificationsCubit; appears in notification center
- [ ] GoRouter DI assessment complete: confirm if GoRouter is registered in GetIt or refactoring needed before NotificationRouteHandler in iter-1; if refactoring needed, create task and plan as Story 1.0
- [ ] dart analyze clean; app_es.arb updated
- [ ] Unit tests: optimistic update + revert on error for FollowCubit; list pagination
- [ ] Widget tests: FollowersListPage, FollowingListPage

stories.story: Como rider, puedo ver el perfil de otro rider y tocar 'Seguir'; el contador de seguidores se incrementa inmediatamente (optimistic update) y el backend registra la relación.
stories.acceptance: Botón cambia a 'Siguiendo' al instante; si el request falla, el estado revierte y muestra snackbar de error; contador correcto al refrescar el perfil.

stories.story: Como rider que ya sigue a alguien, puedo tocar 'Siguiendo', confirmar en un diálogo, y dejar de seguirlo; el contador disminuye de inmediato.
stories.acceptance: Flujo simétrico al de seguir; confirmación requerida para evitar toques accidentales; backend actualiza la relación.

stories.story: Como rider, puedo tocar el número de 'seguidores' o 'siguiendo' en mi perfil y ver una lista con foto, nombre y botón rápido seguir/dejar de seguir a cada uno.
stories.acceptance: Lista pagina correctamente (>20 riders con offset/limit load-more); navegar al rider abre su perfil; empty states visibles ('Aún no tienes seguidores' / 'Aún no sigues a ningún rider').

stories.story: Como rider, mi perfil muestra bio, ciudad, número de seguidores, número de siguiendo, eventos creados y motos registradas (sin datos sensibles).
stories.acceptance: Todos los campos con empty states apropiados; eventos ordenados con próximos primero.

stories.story: Como rider, cuando alguien empieza a seguirme recibo una push 'Te sigue {nombre}' que aparece en el centro de notificaciones.
stories.acceptance: Push en menos de 30s; centro de notificaciones muestra el ítem con badge actualizado; fila insertada en tabla notifications. (Navegación al tocar: iter-1.)

iterations.architectNotes: FollowCubit must be registered as a factory with userId parameter in DI — documented exception to the @injectable singleton pattern. DI module comment must explain this to prevent future developers from marking it as @singleton. PublicVehicleDto must be a separate class from VehicleDto — not VehicleDto with null fields. The distinction matters for Clean Architecture (domain model must not carry null fields representing hidden data).
iterations.designNotes: Follower/following list: quick-follow button must be visually distinguishable from the profile-level follow button. Confirm in frame. Optimistic update visual: follow button must show loading state during API call and revert visually on failure. Deep link domain provisioning is a DevOps action item, not a design concern.
iterations.techLeadNotes: GoRouter DI assessment is a mandatory DoD item. If app_router.dart creates GoRouter as a top-level variable (not DI-registered), NotificationRouteHandler in iter-1 cannot inject it — refactoring will be needed. Assess before iter-4 closes to avoid blocking iter-1.

iterations.goal: Deep Links + Apple Sign-In + Notification Routing: activar el flujo de compartir eventos con Android App Links e iOS Universal Links, añadir Apple Sign-In, y completar el routing de notificaciones push a las pantallas destino.
iterations.notes: Firebase Dynamic Links is EOL — NOT to be used. Domain with TLS + assetlinks.json + apple-app-site-association must be provisioned in iter-4 and verified in iter-1 pre-flight. Story 1.5 completes notification routing for all types from iter-2, iter-3, and iter-4. GoRouter DI: if iter-4 assessment determined refactoring is needed, complete as Story 1.0 before implementing NotificationRouteHandler.
iterations.definitionOfDone:
- [ ] Design gate: share button in EventDetailPage and Apple Sign-In button in login confirmed in frames; Apple Sign-In uses black button with Apple logo (HIG compliance)
- [ ] Pre-flight: com.apple.developer.applesignin entitlement added in Xcode; provisioning profile generated; Apple provider enabled in Firebase Console and Apple Developer Portal
- [ ] Pre-flight: domain verified — curl https://<domain>/.well-known/assetlinks.json and curl https://<domain>/.well-known/apple-app-site-association return 200 with correct content
- [ ] Pre-flight: GoRouter DI refactoring complete if needed (from iter-4 assessment)
- [ ] Backend: GET /api/events/:eventId/share-metadata with Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600
- [ ] Backend (or Firebase Hosting): serves /.well-known/assetlinks.json and /.well-known/apple-app-site-association
- [ ] Backend (or Firebase Hosting): redirect page detects User-Agent and redirects to Play Store / App Store; page shows minimal Rideglory branding (not a bare HTTP redirect)
- [ ] Flutter: app_links ^2.x integrated; AndroidManifest.xml with intent-filter for App Links; iOS Associated Domains entitlement
- [ ] Flutter: sign_in_with_apple ^2.x integrated; button only on iOS (defaultTargetPlatform == TargetPlatform.iOS)
- [ ] Flutter: NotificationRouteHandler in lib/core/services/ — @singleton in DI, registered before GoRouter in main.dart; handles onMessageOpenedApp (background) and getInitialMessage (cold start); graceful error if target entity no longer exists
- [ ] dart analyze clean; app_es.arb updated (share button, error messages)
- [ ] Privacy policy URL in Info.plist
- [ ] Physical device test iOS: cold-start Universal Link + Apple Sign-In
- [ ] Physical device test Android: cold-start App Link
- [ ] All 7 notification types verified routing to correct screens (story 1.5 complete)

stories.story: Como rider, puedo tocar el botón de compartir en el detalle de un evento y el app abre el share sheet nativo con un link listo para enviar.
stories.acceptance: Link generado en menos de 3 segundos; share sheet nativo con preview (nombre del evento + imagen de portada) en tarjeta de WhatsApp.

stories.story: Como rider que recibe un link de evento y tiene la app instalada, al tocar el link la app abre directamente el detalle de ese evento.
stories.acceptance: Navegación funciona desde cold start y background; error apropiado si evento no existe o fue cancelado.

stories.story: Como persona sin la app, al tocar el link en el navegador mobile es redirigida a la Play Store (Android) o App Store (iOS).
stories.acceptance: Redirección correcta en ambas plataformas; página de fallback muestra branding mínimo de Rideglory (no es un HTTP redirect vacío).

stories.story: Como usuario de iPhone, puedo iniciar sesión o registrarme con Apple Sign-In en la pantalla de login.
stories.acceptance: Flujo de auth completo; perfil creado igual que con Google Sign-In; botón solo visible en iOS.

stories.story: Como rider, cuando toco cualquier notificación push la app navega directamente a la pantalla relevante sin pasar por Home.
stories.acceptance: 7 tipos de notificación enrutan correctamente: SOAT → detalle vehículo, inscripción aprobada/rechazada → detalle inscripción, nuevo inscrito → gestión inscritos, mantenimiento → detalle mantenimiento, seguidor → perfil rider. Funciona desde cold start y background. NotificationRouteHandler muestra error apropiado si la entidad destino ya no existe (no crash).

iterations.architectNotes: NotificationRouteHandler must be @singleton in DI, registered in main.dart before GoRouter initialization. GoRouter instance must be injectable — if app_router.dart creates it as a top-level variable, it must be refactored to register in GetIt (scope as Story 1.0 pre-flight if needed from iter-4 assessment). Both onMessageOpenedApp and getInitialMessage streams must be handled.
iterations.designNotes: Apple Sign-In button must follow Apple HIG strictly (black button, white Apple logo, 'Continuar con Apple' text) — App Store review will reject non-compliant implementations. Store redirect fallback page (story 1.3) should show Rideglory logo and a clear CTA, not a blank redirect.
iterations.techLeadNotes: assetlinks.json and apple-app-site-association must be verified reachable (not just deployed) before any App Links / Universal Links code is merged. Test Universal Links with a release build on physical iOS device — debug builds do not honor Associated Domains in the same way. All 7 notification routing types must be tested end-to-end from cold start on physical device before story 1.5 is considered done.

po.focus: iter-2 scoped: SOAT + Notification Foundation + ManageAttendeesPage redesign; 10 stories, 13 tasks, 1 QA task

architect.focus: iter-2 architecture: SOAT (lib/features/soat/ new) + notifications (rebuild stub) full-stack; 6 API contracts; api-gateway first-time Prisma; FCM background isolate pattern; GoRouter DI assessed (static top-level, refactor deferred)

design.focus: iter-2 design complete: SOAT flow (upload+manual+status, 4-state badge), notification center (6 notification types, bell badge), ManageAttendeesPage UPDATE scope confirmed (list+edit, component-swap + state polish). 22 screens/states in 3 HTML mockups. Full UI copy (soat_, notification_ prefixes), component hierarchy, UX flow rules.

backend.focus: iter-2 backend complete: SOAT (vehicles-ms POST/GET /api/vehicles/:vehicleId/soat), fcmToken (users-ms migration), notifications table (api-gateway first-time Prisma), 4 notification endpoints, FCM triggers on registration create/approve/reject, SOAT cron scheduler (30d/7d/day-of America/Bogota). 28 tests pass, 0 TS errors.

frontend.focus: iter-2 frontend complete: SOAT 3-layer feature (upload/manual/status pages, 4-state badge), notifications rebuild (real backend, cursor pagination, markRead/markAllRead optimistic updates), FCM init (firebase_messaging + flutter_local_notifications), NotificationBellButton with unread badge, VehicleSoatSection in vehicle detail. 16 new files. dart analyze 0 issues. 64 tests pass / 1 pre-existing fail (TC-2-28 rider email, unchanged).

qa.focus: iter-2 QA complete: test catalog (TC-2-20 through TC-2-40), dart analyze 0 violations (no regressions), flutter test 64 pass/1 pre-existing fail (no new failures), architecture 8/8 gates passed, sign-off GREEN

devops.focus: iter-2 devops complete: CI validation pass; no YAML changes; firebase_messaging + flutter_local_notifications already in pubspec.yaml; all 12 secrets cover iter-2; docs/DEPLOY.md updated; docs/handoffs/devops.md updated; phase contract generated; ready for tech lead review

tech_lead.focus: iter-2 PR #14 re-reviewed — APPROVED. All 4 blocking violations resolved: AppTextButton used throughout, one widget per file enforced (11 new files extracted), Semantics labels use ARB keys via context.l10n, go_router named routes replace MaterialPageRoute. dart analyze: 0 issues. flutter test: 64 pass / 1 pre-existing fail. PR ready to merge.

events.detail: workflow/state.json initialized. iter-2 and iter-4 already complete per git history.
FRESH mode planning session started. Agents 0-4 pipeline executing.
Plan drafted with 4 iterations (5-8). Status: awaiting_approval. All reviewer feedback incorporated.

FEEDBACK_REPLAN complete. 6 mandatory changes incorporated from Plan Reviewer: (1) Story 6.0 added for Mapbox migration; (2) POST /api/notifications/fcm-token added to Story 5.8 acceptance criteria; (3) Cursor pagination in Stories 5.7 and 5.8; (4) seed.ts requirement added to iter-5 pre-flight; (5) api-gateway prisma init distinguished from 4-service reset; (6) Deep link domain provisioning added as iter-7 action item. docs/PLAN.md and docs/handoffs/planning/04-po-workflow-plan.json updated. Status: awaiting_approval.
Plan drafted (redesign-first replan): 5 iterations (5-9). Iter-5 UI/UX Redesign inserted. Status: awaiting_approval.
Plan approved. 5 iterations locked. Domain skills generated.
Full /iter 1 run starting.

Iteration 1 scoped: UI/UX Redesign — 11 user stories (US-1-1 through US-1-11), 10 tasks (T-1-1 through T-1-10), 2 QA tasks (T-1-8, T-1-9). No backend changes. 5-module PR strategy. Auth frames gate and donut chart scope to be confirmed by design in pre-flight.

Iter-1 architecture confirmed presentation-layer only. Zero rideglory-api / domain / data / DI / router changes. Two design-system primitives specified: AppEventBadge atom (lib/design_system/atoms/badges/, frame zKkmE) and DocumentSlotPill molecule (lib/design_system/molecules/feedback/, frame aGqnv — reusable for iter-2 SOAT). 3-tier color tokenization policy defined (colorScheme → AppColors → status palette). Module PR cadence (5 PRs ≤ 40 files) validated. Slim handoffs delivered to backend (stand-down), frontend, devops (no-op), qa. DIAGRAMS.md initialized.
Design phase active: gap analysis performed, 5 HTML mockup modules produced, UI copy and component hierarchy documented.
